title: Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: HerniaSurge International Guidelines for Inguinal Hernia

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: Prenatal US for diagnosis; postnatal CXR confirms; NGT placement shows stomach position
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: Gas-filled bowel in hemithorax, mediastinal shift to contralateral side, hypoplastic lung
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Stabilize (gentle ventilation, NO bag-mask), NGT decompression → surgical repair after stabilization
